name: Herman Autrey
abstract: Herman Autrey (December 4, 1904 – June 14, 1980) was an American jazz trumpeter.

about_quotes.speaker: Snub Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Now, I got George Thigpen in that band; do you remember him? Herman Autrey? [INTERVIEWEE] Which band? [INTERVIEWER] Fletcher's band. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, yeah. That's why I remember those name so well.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: I used to go up to New Hampshire and do a private thing for them with guys like Herb Hall and Cheatham, Herman Autry Doc and those guys.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Davern
about_quotes.evidence: a lot of the blacks that I played with who were encouraging to me, Dickie Wells, Rex Stewart, Claude Hopkins, Herman Autrey
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Davern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: in 1969 I took a trip with Red Richards, Saints and Sinners, back to Europe again, and we had Buddy Tate and Herman Autrey.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: So the cat sitting next to you might have been a cat like Herman Autrey, he says, man, don't tell him you don't drink.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: he would bring up people like Herb Hall and McK. Dickenson and Herman Autrey and Red Richards
